nodes.label: AI for communication refinement
nodes.grounding_span: if I have something I need to express to another co-worker that may not be the best news, I use AI to help me figure out a way to 'break the news' in a more positive or less harsh way

nodes.label: AI as idea generator and organizer
nodes.grounding_span: I will dump a summary of most or all of the ideas and let the AI work with it/organize them

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool
nodes.grounding_span: it helped me clean up my schedule in an extremely organized way in a matter of minutes

nodes.label: Personal control over work output
nodes.grounding_span: providing results that are 100% mine, not influenced or effected by outside sources

nodes.label: AI delegation for simple tasks
nodes.label_negative: Full reliance on AI leading to skill atrophy
nodes.grounding_span: I do not fully rely on AI to do every task or job

nodes.label: AI adoption in workplace
nodes.label_negative: Open AI use in an AI-friendly workplace
nodes.grounding_span: I definitely keep it more private, unless I have a great breakthrough

nodes.label: Effective communication and relationship building

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for scheduling
nodes.grounding_span: I was about 4 hours deep at that point and was so happy

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: incredibly frustrated with myself for spending so much time instead of getting help

nodes.label: Intentional boundary on AI use for easy tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle my 'easy' tasks on my own

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I think my relationship with AI at work will continue to evolve and flourish

nodes.label: Delegation with verification — offloads complex tasks but double-checks results

nodes.label: Selective disclosure — compartmentalizes AI use based on social context
